SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF ROLANDO PASGUALI IN SUPPORT OF MOTIONS TO APPOINT ARBITRATOR AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS FILED BY PETITIONER DOTSON-LINDO, NARRISA
ORDER APPOINTING HON. MARGARET KEMP AS ARBITRATOR.

DISCOVERY 610, PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR DISCOVERY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. RESPONDENT IS PRECLUDED FROM OFFERING IN ANY PROCEEDING, EITHER DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY THROUGH THE USE OF AN EXPERT WITNESS, ANY EVIDENCE OF PETITIONER'S STATEMENT OF DECEMBER 6, 2001. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT REPEATEDLY MADE EVASIVE RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR THE SPECIFIED DISCOVERY. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $4885 ARE TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn For Sanctions Based On Failure To Provide Discovery & False Responses To Discovery By State Farm Insuance
Off Calendar May-26-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
ROLANDO PASQUALI, ESQ. FOR PETITIONER/MOVING PARTY, NARRISA DOTSON-LINDO
ROBERT M. PAVE, ESQ. AND DENISE K. TOP, ESQ. FOR RESPONDENT/OPPOSING PARTY, STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY

MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. Petitioner's motion for discovery sanctions is granted in part. Respondent is precluded from offering in any proceeding, either directly or indirectly through the use of an expert witness, any evidence of Petitioner's statement of December 6, 2001. The Court finds that defendant repeatedly made evasive responses to requests for the specified discovery. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $4885 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (610/BEC) COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
